headline: The US and Israel are fighting different wars against the same foe
description: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu share many objectives. However, their respective public opinions and electoral interests may ultimately highlight significant differences between the two leaders.

headline: EU Energy Commissioner Dan Jorgensen: 'Capping gas prices is a possibility'
In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
French prime minister pushes on as 2025 public deficit beats forecasts
description: France's public deficit stood at 5.1% of GDP in 2025, beating the target of 5.4%, according to data released Friday. The positive surprise boosts the credibility of the 5% target in 2026, despite the oil shock.

headline: Napoleon's long-lost hat to go on display
description: Made by Poupard, Napoleon's official hatmaker, the headpiece displays all the hallmark features of an authentic imperial bicorne, including its distinctive proportions, a small tricolor cockade and a silk-taffeta lining. It will be exhibited at the Chateau de Chantilly, north of Paris, later in the year.

headline: Cybercrime, the blind spot in museum security
description: Dozens of online ticketing systems in France's cultural sector were shut down following a large-scale cyberattack targeting Vivaticket, the leading operator in the field, on March 2. French institutions are still reviewing their defense systems.

headline: 'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
description: The American president's disappointments and contradictory statements on the conflict in Iran obscure the key question of how to end the war, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

headline: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
description: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

headline: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
description: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.
